claims.summary: `FEATURE_ABS_MAX` can flatten high-intensity warmth/tension peaks unless raw overflow is preserved somewhere visible.
claims.rationale: The codec overflow report preserves raw pre-bound values, clipped dims, overflow amount, overflow ratio, and bounded delivery status without changing live ceilings.

claims.summary: Tanh plus hard clamping can create dead zones where subtle high-pressure shifts are no longer distinguishable.
claims.rationale: Overflow and tail-headroom tests distinguish ordinary emotional clipping from tail/vibrancy headroom and quiet no-overflow cases.

claims.summary: Dynamic ceiling changes or a new pressure-gradient feature could improve peak expressivity but would alter live semantic vector behavior.
claims.rationale: The default-off follow-up hook names future emotional aperture work, but dynamic ceilings or new live vector dimensions remain a separate Tier 4/5 approval route.
